Fibonacci levels help you see where momentum pauses by mapping potential areas of support & resistance using ratios from the Fib sequence.  

I find them useful because strong trends often consolidate at these levels before continuing higher & this list reflects names consolidating between the 0.382  & 0.618 extension levels.

Stanley Druckenmiller says his edge isn’t raw IQ but it’s the ability to “pull the trigger” decisively when conviction is high.

He wasn’t top of his class but he believes his success comes from a narrow, obsessive intelligence tailored specifically to the investing game.
$RKLB launched its HASTE 7 hypersonic test mission from Virginia dubbed “That’s Not A Knife”

Stanley Druckenmiller sat down with Morgan Stanley to discuss how he’d build a portfolio from scratch today.

He also explains why contrarianism is overrated and which stock he regrets selling too early.

OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said AI infrastructure spending only works if new revenue flows into ecosystem after responding to a question about “circular financing.”

$AMZN CEO Andy Jassy also added that its planned ~$200B 2026 CapEx is backed by strong demand signals and clear visibility to monetization over the next 18–24 months.

We’re in the 1880s of AI where the technology exists and adoption is spreading, but the real productivity transformation hasn’t hit yet.

That gap between "invention" and "transformation" is exactly where investors lose patience.. right before the gains show up.

OpenAI has 900M weekly users and 50M+ subscribers while Claude serves ~19M monthly users but generates far higher revenue per user driven by enterprise API demand.

OpenAI clearly won consumer mindshare but Anthropic is winning enterprise revenue.

South Korea just cleared $GOOGL Maps to operate fully in-country.

After years of restrictions on exporting high-precision data, Google can finally offer real navigation in one of the most tech-dense markets in the world.

OK we rev from targets as posted rules are to bank & work runners they to stop. Now we have bear fg in build SPY QQQ IWM only 2 out comes we play or fail be nimble hard stops in place just in the $ as to rules!

📺 THIS IS NOT A BREAKOUT MARKET

Earlier in the week, the market looked like it might finally break out of its range.

Then everything flipped with $NVDA earnings.

#Nvidia broke below the critical key $194 level. Once that failed, tech rolled over hard and dragged the broader market down with it. 

$SPY failed to hold $690 – its critical level – and quickly flushed to $684, stopping out traders who had positioned for continuation. It was a failed setup.

Now what? We’re back in the same frustrating range.

The key questions for Friday:
– Does $SPY hold 684?
– Does $QQQ hold the $603–$604 area?
– Can we hold yesterday’s lows and turn green for a tactical bounce?

If yes, we can make some money. If not, the range remains heavy and downside risk expands.

This is still a range-bound market. Range trades mean you take money when you have it — you don’t overstay.

It is frustrating, yes. But ranges eventually resolve. Until then, discipline and levels matter more than any predictions.
$SPX futures were -30 as 6915 failed to hold, and now we see if 6859 (yesterday’s low) holds or not. Tricky action—lots of range-type trades, but no real traction.

It's gonna be AI fam.

There are many many categories of subprime this cycle.
0% interest rates for a decade does that.
It's not just private credit. It's everywhere.

The 10-year just broke below 4%.

Equities own the narrative.

Duration owns the asymmetry.

If growth cools from here, the most hated trade in the world becomes the most profitable.

Few understand this.

Private credit is this cycle’s subprime.

Blue Owl froze redemptions.
BDCs are cutting dividends.
Software accounts for 40% of PE-backed loans.

And AI is about to gut the collateral.

JPMorgan says they’re “watching closely.”

That’s what they said in 2007 too.
